title: Update working memory from STT onboarding auth session
summary: Durable context from the STT onboarding 401 fix, including the local onboarding auth bypass scope and rejected broader approaches.

claims.text: The onboarding and settings STT test path uses transcribeAudio() to POST recorded audio to /api/sessions/{session_key}/upload with transcribe=true, and the web router exposes that route as the session_upload HTTP endpoint, so STT testing goes through HTTP auth middleware rather than only WebSocket RPC.

In auth_gate's Unauthorized branch, a local request on an onboarding-bypass path receives Loopback identity only when onboarding wizard_status explicitly reports onboarded=false; if wizard_status fails or omits onboarded, the middleware defaults to onboarded=true and continues normal unauthorized handling.

The local onboarding auth bypass is limited to /api/sessions/, /api/bootstrap, /api/gon, /api/tailscale/, and /ws/ paths; privileged APIs such as /api/config remain outside the bypass and must still authenticate even during onboarding.

The session rejected making NoopOnboardingService::wizard_status return onboarded=true for the auth bypass because the web UI onboarding redirect logic also treats a missing onboarded field as not onboarded; the safe auth-specific default belongs in auth_gate instead.

The session rejected a broad local onboarding bypass for every /api/* or /ws/* unauthorized request after review; the accepted contract is a narrow allowlist for wizard-required paths so privileged endpoints stay protected.

metadata.reason: The transcript records the initial plan to add onboarded=true to NoopOnboardingService, the resulting chat_ui onboarding test failure, and the accepted fix of reverting the noop change while defaulting auth_gate's missing onboarded value to true.

The transcript records PR review feedback that the original /api/* and /ws/* local onboarding bypass was too broad and the follow-up change narrowed it to wizard-required paths with a privileged-endpoint regression test.
